1.site: Human gut virome, adult healthy (Western diet)
1.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Overwhelmingly phage-dominated (~85-95%); eukaryotic virus low single digits.
1.expected_na_type: Predominantly dsDNA (tailed phages) + ssDNA (Microviridae); RNA minor.
1.plausibility_verdict: PLAUSIBLE / GOOD. phage 88.5% / euk 3% / DNA 90.3% match consensus. Top family Suoliviridae is a valid crAss-like Crassvirales family. No red flags.

citations.title: The human gut virome: composition, colonization, interactions, and impacts on human health.
citations.supports: Healthy adult gut virome dominated by bacteriophages, predominantly Crassvirales (crAss-like) in industrialized populations; large dark-matter fraction.

citations.title: A Previously Undescribed Highly Prevalent Phage Identified in a Danish Enteric Virome Catalog.
citations.supports: Gut viromes of 91 healthy subjects dominated by phages; crAss-like phage among the two most prevalent worldwide.

10.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Still phage-dominated; disease signal is a phage-community shift (Caudovirales expansion), not eukaryotic takeover.
10.expected_na_type: Predominantly DNA (dsDNA tailed phages + ssDNA Microviridae).
10.disease_signature: Hallmark is EXPANSION of Caudovirales bacteriophages (Crohn's and UC) with decreased viral evenness/diversity; disease- and cohort-specific (Norman 2015; Zuo 2019).
10.plausibility_verdict: PLAUSIBLE. phage 80% / euk 10% / DNA 90% keeps correct structure; top family Demerecviridae (Caudoviricetes) consistent with Caudovirales-expansion signature. Caveat: primary lit reports order-level Caudovirales; the modest euk rise is within-noise, not a validated signature.

citations.title: Disease-specific alterations in the enteric virome in inflammatory bowel disease.
citations.supports: CD and UC associated with significant expansion of Caudovirales bacteriophages; not secondary to bacterial changes.

citations.title: Gut mucosal virome alterations in ulcerative colitis.
citations.supports: UC shows expansion of Caudovirales bacteriophages and decreased mucosal viral diversity/richness/evenness.

11.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Shift toward INCREASED eukaryotic-virus abundance/richness (immunodeficiency-driven); phages remain present; euk fraction markedly higher than healthy.
11.expected_na_type: Expanded eukaryotic viruses of interest are DNA (Adenoviridae, Anelloviridae, Papillomaviridae); phages DNA; DNA-dominated.
11.disease_signature: Low CD4 associated with expansion of enteric eukaryotic DNA viruses: adenovirus (Monaco 2016); Anelloviridae/Adenoviridae/Papillomaviridae in CD4<200, anellovirus predicting poor immune recovery, declining after ART (Boukadida 2024).
11.plausibility_verdict: PLAUSIBLE in DIRECTION, with annotation-quality flag. euk 19% (vs ~3% healthy) captures the expansion; DNA 71% consistent. BUT 29.3% unknown-family and top=Unknown obscure the diagnostic Adenoviridae/Anelloviridae/Papillomaviridae taxa; discriminating eukaryotic viruses appear under-annotated. RNA=0 acceptable (signature taxa are DNA).

citations.title: Altered Virome and Bacterial Microbiome in Human Immunodeficiency Virus-Associated Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome.
citations.supports: Low CD4 associated with expansion of enteric adenovirus sequences, independent of ART.

citations.title: Alterations of the gut microbiome in HIV infection highlight human anelloviruses as potential predictors of immune recovery.
citations.supports: Expansion of Anelloviridae/Adenoviridae/Papillomaviridae in stool of people with HIV and CD4<200; anellovirus expansion predicts poor immune recovery.

8.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Bacteriophage-dominated (Caudovirales + Microviridae); very high diversity and interindividual variation; large uncharacterized fraction.
8.expected_na_type: Predominantly DNA (Caudovirales dsDNA + Microviridae ssDNA); minor eukaryotic RNA.
8.plausibility_verdict: PARTLY PLAUSIBLE, flag on phage/unknown balance. Large unknown share defensible (real dark matter), BUT mouse gut is phage-DOMINATED; observed phage 36.7% with unknown-host 58.6% and top=Unknown inverts expectation. Likely Caudovirales/Microviridae phages sitting in the unknown pool. RNA 6.2% acceptable.

citations.title: Dynamic alterations of the mice gut virome after Coxsackievirus B3 infection.
citations.supports: Mouse gut virome dominated by Caudovirales and Microviridae.

citations.title: Impact of antibiotic perturbation on fecal viral communities in mice.
citations.supports: Wild-type C57BL/6J fecal virome shows high diversity and interindividual variation; eukaryotic viruses and bacteriophages.

15.expected_phage_vs_eukaryote: Eukaryotic-RNA-virus-dominated by design; RNA phages minor; an enteric-pathogen RNA virome legitimately near-0% phage.
15.plausibility_verdict: PLAUSIBLE / GOOD. euk 90.8% / phage 0 / RNA 90.8% correct. Top Caliciviridae (norovirus) reasonable, though cohort-dependent (Picornaviridae, Reoviridae/Rotavirus often equally abundant). No red flags.

citations.title: Metagenomic Analysis of the Enteric RNA Virome of Infants from the Oukasie Clinic, North West Province, South Africa, Reveals Diverse Eukaryotic Viruses.
citations.supports: Enteric RNA virome dominated by diverse eukaryotic RNA viruses: Reoviridae/Rotavirus, Picornaviridae, Astroviridae, Caliciviridae/norovirus.

citations.title: Characterization of Norovirus and Other Human Enteric Viruses in Sewage and Stool Samples Through Next-Generation Sequencing.
citations.supports: Human enteric RNA viruses from stool/sewage include norovirus, rotavirus, enterovirus, astrovirus, sapovirus.
